tweets.title: R to @Amit_Narang: Kenya
tweets.content:
Kenya

India in Kenya (@IndiainKenya)

12th International Day of Yoga in Nairobi🧘‍♀️🧘‍♂️

Over 2,500 participants came together at the Oshwal Centre Grounds this morning to celebrate the 12th International Day of Yoga in Nairobi under the theme ‘Yoga for Healthy Ageing’.

The event was graced by the Under Secretary General and Director General of the United Nations Office at Nairobi as Chief Guest and brought together members of the diplomatic corps, officials of Government of Kenya, the business community, the Indian diaspora and yoga enthusiasts from across Kenya.

The celebrations featured captivating Rhythmic Yoga and Stick Yoga performances before participants joined in the Common Yoga Protocol, showcasing the myriad physical health benefits and mental wellbeing that yoga embodies.

Ayurveda stalls at the venue also offered participants an opportunity to learn more about India’s alternative system of medicine that complements the holistic approach to health and well-being.

The large participation reflected the growing popularity of yoga in Kenya and the enduring bonds of friendship between 🇮🇳 and 🇰🇪.

#IDY2026 #YogaForHealthyAgeing

tweets.title: Russia has reportedly begun operational deployment of the Volna Kupol Garant electronic warfare system, designed to disrupt Starlink satellite communications used for drone control, reconnaissance, and battlefield coordination. According to reports, the system jams signals in the 14–14.5 GHz band by transmitting powerful interference toward Starlink satellites. One system is said to cover an area of roughly 20 sq km and is intended to protect high-value assets such as military bases, bridges, and energy infrastructure. Russian authorities have not officially commented on the reported deployment.

tweets.title: This is how Su30 fleet was created over the years : 📅 30 Nov 1996 – 40 aircraft (Russia) 📅 18 Dec 1998 – 10 aircraft (Russia) 📅 28 Dec 2000 – 140 aircraft (HAL) 📅 10 Oct 2007 – 40 aircraft (HAL) 📅 24 Dec 2012 – 42 aircraft (HAL) After that, there was a gap of over a decade. 📅 15 Sep 2023 – AoN for 12 replacement aircraft 📅 12 Dec 2024 – ₹13,500 crore contract signed with HAL for 12 Su-30MKIs A textbook example of how not to manage a procurement. OEM that forgot about upgrades, users who weren’t looking for updates (no one was bothered about upgrade) because Su57 was coming as FGFA.
tweets.content:
This is how Su30 fleet was created over the years :

📅 30 Nov 1996 – 40 aircraft (Russia)
📅 18 Dec 1998 – 10 aircraft (Russia)
📅 28 Dec 2000 – 140 aircraft (HAL)
📅 10 Oct 2007 – 40 aircraft (HAL)
📅 24 Dec 2012 – 42 aircraft (HAL)

After that, there was a gap of over a decade.

📅 15 Sep 2023 – AoN for 12 replacement aircraft
📅 12 Dec 2024 – ₹13,500 crore contract signed with HAL for 12 Su-30MKIs

A textbook example of how not to manage a procurement. OEM that forgot about upgrades, users who weren’t looking for updates (no one was bothered about upgrade) because Su57 was coming as FGFA.

tweets.title: Many wonder why Rafale’s OSF uses two apertures while Eurofighter’s PIRATE packs most functions into a single IRST turret. The answer lies in the era they were designed. In the 1980s and early 1990s, air forces placed significant emphasis on long-range visual identification before weapons release. Rafale’s OSF therefore combined a dedicated IRST with a separate high-resolution visual identification channel. As sensor technology matured, modern IRSTs became capable of extracting far more information from infrared imagery itself, reducing the need for a dedicated visual-ID sensor. That’s why the more integrated PIRATE approach appears more elegant today. Initial units of typhoon did not had PIRATE, it came later. Different design philosophies shaped by different operational requirements and not necessarily a case of one being better than the other. However OSF forces Rafale to have a smaller radar aperture.
tweets.content:
Many wonder why Rafale’s OSF uses two apertures while Eurofighter’s PIRATE packs most functions into a single IRST turret.

The answer lies in the era they were designed. In the 1980s and early 1990s, air forces placed significant emphasis on long-range visual identification before weapons release. Rafale’s OSF therefore combined a dedicated IRST with a separate high-resolution visual identification channel.

As sensor technology matured, modern IRSTs became capable of extracting far more information from infrared imagery itself, reducing the need for a dedicated visual-ID sensor. That’s why the more integrated PIRATE approach appears more elegant today. Initial units of typhoon did not had PIRATE, it came later.

Different design philosophies shaped by different operational requirements and not necessarily a case of one being better than the other. However OSF forces Rafale to have a smaller radar aperture.
